title: uuf2inst
text: uuf2inst Copy a basic parameter or entity field of type handle to a program variable. Explain this code long uuf2inst (UHACThOpr, intseqNr, UHACT*hInst); Copy Parameters hOpr Handle to the operation or entity parameter seqNr Sequence number of the basic parameter or entity field (sequence numbers start with 1) hInst Instance handle (Output) Return Values See the "Call-In API Return Codes" topic. Use Library: ucall Description uuf2inst copies the value from a basic parameter or entity field of type handle to a program variable of type UHACT . This function can be used to pass instance handles from 4GL to 3GL. Note that you need to initialize hInst before calling uuf2inst . The value should either be zero, or a valid handle. In the latter case, uuf2inst will free it before assigning a new value. If you fail to initialize hInst , your program is likely to crash. The following example shows how to activate a service by using its instance handle. First we activate the operation RUNSRV2. This service will create a new instance of another service, SERV2, and pass back the instance handle of this instance as an out parameter. The 3GL code picks up that handle, instantiates an operation in it, and activates that operation. The operation RUNSERV2 in service TESTSVC is defined as follows: Explain this code operation runserv2 params handle hServ : out endparams newinstance "SERV2", hServ if ( $status < 0 ) hServ = 0 endif end Copy The operation HELLO in service SERV2 is defined as follows: Explain this code operation hello putmess "Hello from SERV2.hello() !!!" end Copy This is the 3GL code: Explain this code #include <h3gl.h> test_handle () { UHACT hEnv, hIn1, hIn2; /* Environment and instance handles */ UHACT hOp1, hOp2; /* Operation handles */ long actStatus, procErr; /* Activation status and procerr */ ueopen(&hEnv); /* Get environment handle */ /* Instantiate service TESTSVC */ uinstnew (hEnv, (UCHAR *)"TESTSVC", 0, 0, UDEFAULT_COMM_MODE, 0, &hIn1, &procErr); /* Instantiate operation RUNSERV2 */ uinstopr(hIn1, (UCHAR *)"RUNSERV2", 0, &hOp1); /* Activate operation RUNSERV2 */ uopract (hOp1, &actStatus, &procErr); hIn2 = 0; /* Initialize handle */ uuf2inst(hOp1, 1, &hIn2); /* Get instance handle for SERV2 */ /* Instantiate operation HELLO */ uinstopr (hIn2, (UCHAR *)"HELLO", 0, &hOp2); uopract (hOp2, &actStatus, &procErr);/* Activate operation HELLO */ uinstdel (hIn1, 0); /* Destroy instance */ ufreeh (&hOp1); /* Free RUNSERV2 operation handle*/ ufreeh (&hOp2); /* Free HELLO operation handle */ ufreeh (&hIn1); /* Free instance handle */ ufreeh (&hIn2); /* Free instance handle */ ufreeh (&hEnv); /* Free environment handle */ } Copy Related Links Call-In API Return Codes Codes that can possibly be returned by functions from the 3GL call-in API are consistent with the values returned by the ProcScript function $procerror, unless noted otherwise. uinst2uf Copy an instance handle to a parameter or entity field of type handle.
